# Governance Directives

Follow these directives in every file you write or edit.

## Non-Negotiable Constraints

These are invariants. Violation is never acceptable.

_No invariants captured yet. Run `/edikt:invariant:new` to capture a hard constraint._

## Routing Table

Before starting a task, scan this table. If your task matches any signals or scopes, read the referenced file.

| Signals | Scope | File |
|---|---|---|
| `.kt`/`.kts` files, Kotlin idioms, null safety, `data class`, sealed types, coroutines, `Dispatchers`, Spring constructor injection, Axon commands/events | implementation, review | `governance/kotlin.md` |

## Reminders

Before acting, check the relevant constraint.

[edikt:reminders:start]: #
- Before declaring a Kotlin variable → reach for `val` first; use `var` only if reassignment is required by control flow (ref: kotlin)
- Before catching an exception → catch the specific subtype, never `Exception` / `Throwable` / `RuntimeException` in business logic (ref: kotlin)
[edikt:reminders:end]: #

## Verification Checklist

Before finishing, verify each item. If any fails, fix before submitting.

- [ ] No `!!` non-null assertions outside test code (`grep -RInE '!!' src/main`) (ref: kotlin)
- [ ] No `GlobalScope.launch` / `GlobalScope.async` in application code (`grep -RIn 'GlobalScope' src/main`) (ref: kotlin)
- [ ] No Spring field injection via `@Autowired lateinit var` (`grep -RInE '@Autowired[[:space:]]+lateinit' src/main`) (ref: kotlin)

## Reminder: Non-Negotiable Constraints

These constraints were listed above and are restated for emphasis.
Do not violate them under any circumstances.

_No invariants captured yet._
